details.label: IMPECT
details.definition:
In the current version, the 'IMPECT' score includes all 13 Packing KPIs as well as goals & assists.

These KPIs affect the 'IMPECT' score in the way they are empirically able to increase or decrease the probability of scoring or conceding a goal. For example 'Bypassed Defenders' is weighted stronger than 'Bypassed Opponents', as it empirically has a higher influence on the goal scoring probability. The current version of the 'IMPECT' score, is not yet based on the Packing Expected Threat model.
details.meaning: Overall performance of a player; their influence on goals scored & conceded in total.

details.label: IMPECT (without goals)
details.definition:
This score is similar to the 'IMPECT' score, as it includes all 13 Packing KPIs & assists. This score however excludes 'Goals'.

The performance of a player is measured excluding their goals scored. Since goals have an influence on the 'IMPECT' score and are not used here, the value 'IMPECT (without goals)' is consequently more robust over small sample sizes than the 'IMPECT' score. With goalscoring removed from the score, this score evaluates other KPIs higher.
details.meaning: Overall performance of a player not taking into account goals scored.

details.label: IMPECT (with post-shot xG)
details.definition:
This score is similar to the 'IMPECT' score as it includes all 13 Packing KPIs and assists. This score however excludes 'Goals' and uses 'post-shot XG' instead.

Post-shot xG measures a player's shot placement. If a player's shot has a post-shot xG value of 0.35 but still scores 1 goal, the post-shot xG value of 0.35 is used in this score. When considering a player's performance this makes sense to include because he can only influence his shot, which is reflected by the post-shot xG value. Whether this shot then leads to a goal is largely dependent on the opponent's goalkeeping performance. As each shot is evaluated by a precise post-shot xG value instead of goals that are only '1' = goal' or '0' = no goal', the 'IMPECT (with post-shot xG)' score is more robust than the 'IMPECT' score.
details.meaning: Overall performance of a player taking into account the quality of their finishing and not simply their goals.

details.label: Offensive IMPECT
details.definition:
The current 'Offensive IMPECT' score includes all offensive Packing scores as well as goals & assists. These scores affect the 'Offensive IMPECT' score in the way they empirically increase or decrease the probability of scoring or conceding a goal. This for example means 'Bypassed Defenders' is weighted stronger than 'Bypassed Opponents', as it empirically has a higher influence on the goal scoring probability. Therefore, the current version of the 'Offensive IMPECT' score, is not yet based on the Packing Expected Threat model.

The following KPIs are part of the 'Offensive IMPECT' score:
  • Minutes / Goal (positive)
  • Goals (positive)
  • Missed Chances / Goal (negative)
  • Minutes / Assist (positive)
  • Assists (positive)
  • Bypassed Defenders (positive)
  • Bypassed Opponents (positive)
  • Turnover Removed Teammates (negative)
  • Bypassed Defenders Receiving (positive)
  • Bypassed Opponents Receiving (positive)
  • Ratio Turnover Added Opponents (negative)
  • Ratio Backpass Added Opponents (negative)

    The empirically developed weightings of these KPIs apply across all positions. Therefore, a goal by a centre-back counts just as much as a goal by a striker, as it generates the same effect on the game. Equal actions are therefore weighted the same, regardless of the position in which the player played.
details.meaning: Offensive performance of a player; their influence on the scored & conceded goals.

details.label: Defensive IMPECT
details.definition:
The current 'Defensive IMPECT' score includes all defensive Packing KPIs as well as goals & assists. These KPIs affect the 'Defensive IMPECT' score in the way they empirically increase or decrease the probability of scoring or conceding a goal. This for example means 'Bypassed Defenders' is weighted stronger than 'Bypassed Opponents', as it empirically has a higher influence on the goal scoring probability. Therefore, the current version of the 'Defensive IMPECT'-score, is not yet based on the Packing Expected Threat model.

The following KPI's are part of the 'Defenisve IMPECT' score:
  • Ratio Offensive Interventions / Suffered Bypassed Opponents (positive)
  • Ratio Added Teammates / Suffered Bypassed Opponents (positive)
  • Interventions Removed Opponents Defenders (positive)
  • Suffered Bypassed Defenders (Team) (negative)
  • Suffered Bypassed Players (Team) (negative)
  • (+/-) Suffered Bypassed Defenders (negative)
  • Influence on Allowed Chances (negative)

    The empirically developed weightings of the KPIs apply across all positions. Therefore, 1 removed opponent (defender) with a intervention by a centre-back counts just as much as 1 removed opponent (defender) with a intervention by a striker, as it generates the same effect on the game. Equal actions are therefore weighted the same, regardless of the position in which the player played.

This combines KPIs related to scoring. The following KPIs make up the Scorer Score:
  • Goals (positive)
  • Assists (positive)
  • Ball Loss Added Opponents by Action Shot (negative)
  • Ball Loss Added Opponents Defenders by Action Shot (negative)
details.meaning: The efficiency in front of the goal; goals & assists minus the consequence of missed goal scoring chances; this therefore shows a player's effectiveness in goalscoring situations.

details.definition:
This combines offensive KPIs. The following KPIs make up the Offensive Score:

  • Bypassed Opponents (positive)
  • Bypassed Defenders (positive)
  • Ball Loss Removed Teammates (negative)
  • Ball Loss Removed Teammates Defenders (negative)

    The Offensive Score is made up of these 4 Packing KPIs & does not take into account any later game phases from the Packing+ dataset.
details.meaning: The efficiency of a player's offensive play in possession.

details.label: Receiving Score
details.definition:
This combines KPIs related to receiving & controling the ball. The following KPIs make up the Receiving Score:
  • Bypassed Defenders Receiving (positive)
  • Bypassed Opponents Receiving (positive)
  • Reverse Play Added Opponents (negative)
  • Reverse Play Added Opponents Defenders (negative)
  • Ball Loss Added Opponents (negative)
  • Ball Loss Added Opponents Defenders (negative)
details.meaning: Efficiency as a pass receiver, impact on the probability to score goals, verticality, movement between the lines, ball control. A score of 0.5 means that the sum of all pass-receiving-KPIs equals a half goal per game

details.label: Interventions Score
details.definition:
This combines all KPIs related to interventions. The following KPIs make up the Intervention Score:

  • Ratio Off.Interv/Suff. Byp. (positive)
  • Ratio Add.Teamm/Suff. Byp. (positive)
  • Interv. Rem. Opp. Def. (positive)

    An intervention is any action on the ball, immediately after the opposition have been in possession (interception, ground duel etc...).
details.meaning: The efficiency of a player's effectiveness in making interventions; both from an offensive perspective (Ratio Offensive Interventions/Suffered Bypassed Players & Intervention Removed Opponent Defenders) & defensive perspective (Ratio Added Teammates/Suffered Bypassed Players).

details.label: Defensive Score
details.definition:
This combines KPIs related to defensive positioning. The efficiency of a player's defensive performance, inferring their defensive positional play, by using 'top-down metrics' (Suffered Bypassed Defenders (Team), Suffered Bypassed Players (Team) & Influence on Allowed Chances) and seeing a team's defensive performance whilst a player is on the pitch. The following KPIs make up the Defensive Score:
  • Suffered Bypassed Defenders (Team) (negative)
  • Suffered Bypassed Players (Team) (negative)
  • (+/-) Suffered Bypassed Defenders (negative)
  • Influence on Allowed Chances (negative)
details.meaning: The efficiency as a defender of space and specifically the spaces immediately in front of the goal.

details.label: Offensive IMPECT (without goals)
details.definition: This score is similar to the 'Offensive IMPECT' score. This score however excludes 'Goals'.
details.meaning: Offensive performance of a player; their influence on the scored & conceded goals not taking into account goals scored.

details.label: Offensive IMPECT (with post-shot xG)
details.definition: This score is similar to the 'Offensive IMPECT' score. This score however excludes 'Goals' and uses 'post-shot XG' instead.
details.meaning: Offensive performance of a player; their influence on the scored & conceded goals taking into account the quality of their finishing and not simply their goals.

details.label: Offensive Ball Control
details.definition: Sum of all opponents who have been previously bypassed and now are back in the game after losing ball possession (without shots)
details.meaning: Number of ball losses in the offense, destroying promising situations in the offense, can an offensive player remain ball possession upfront?. When an Offensive Midfielder loses the ball in front of the defensive row (5 opponents left) he gets a (negative) credit for 6 added opponents through turnover

details.label: Ratio - remove opponents
details.definition:
Offensive interventions in context to potential interventions, meaningful ratio for the KPI "Interception - removed opponents"

Ratio of "Interception - removed opponents" (numerator) to "Suffered bypassing" (denominator), since balls can be conquered offensively only if the opponent tries to bypass the own team.

The formula is:

"Interception - removed opponents" / ("Suffered bypassing (team value with player on the field)"
details.meaning: Offensive interventions removing opponents

details.label: Ratio - remove opponents defenders
details.definition:
Offensive ball conquests in context to potential possible interventions, meaningful ratio for the KPI "Interventions - removed opponents (defenders)". Defenders are the last 5 field players in the respective game situation plus the goalkeeper.

Ratio of "Interception - removed opponents (defenders)" (numerator) to "Suffered bypassing" (denominator), because you can conquer balls offensively only if the opponent tries to bypass your own team.

The formula is:

"Interception - removed opponents (defenders)" / ("Suffered bypassing (team value with player on the field)"
details.meaning: Very offensive interventions, with which at least one opponent defender (last 5 field players) was removed

details.label: Ratio - add teammates
details.definition:
Defensive interventions in context to potential interventions, meaningful ratio for the KPI "Interventions - added teammates".

All 10 own field players in the respective game situation plus the goalkeeper are considered as teammates. A player can also bring himself back into the game by winning the ball or clearing the ball; the player himself is also considered a "teammate" here.

Ratio of "Interventions - added teammates" (numerator) to own defenders bypassed (denominator), since only as many own players can be added back into the game as were previously bypassed. Players can be taken out of the game in two ways: By losing the ball themselves and by being bypassed by the opponent.

The formula is:

"Interventions - added teammates" /("Suffered bypassing (team value with player on the field)"+ "Turnover - removed teammates (team value while player on pitch)")
details.meaning: Defensive interventions & clearances when own players were bypassed; rate of defensive interventions that added own teammates back into the game, winning defensively important balls.

details.label: Ratio - add teammates defenders
details.definition:
Defensive interventions in context to potential possible interventions, meaningful ratio for the KPI "Interventions - added teammates (defender)".

Defenders are the last 5 field players in the respective game situation plus the goalkeeper. A player can also bring himself back into the game by winning the ball or clearing the ball, also the player himself is considered as "teammate" here.

Ratio of "Interventions - added teammates (defender)" (numerator) to own defenders taken out of the game (denominator), since only as many own defenders can be brought back into the game as were previously taken out of the game. Players can be taken out of the game in two ways: By losing the ball themselves and when they are bypassed by the opponent.
details.meaning: Defensive interventions & clearances in the last line; Ratio of defensive interventions with which own teammates and here even defenders (last 5 field players) were added back into play; clearing of very dangerous situations.

details.label: +/- Suffered Bypassed Defenders
details.definition:
Bypassed own defenders in context to players taken out of the game, taking into account the team's values in the competition when the player was not on the field, in order to be able to measure the influence of the player on own defence broken. The output is a deviation from the competition average.

Defenders are the last 5 field players in the respective match situation plus the goalkeeper. A player can also be a defender himself if he is one of the last 5 field players in the respective match situation.

Ratio of "Suffered bypassed defenders" (numerator) to removed teammates (denominator), since the stability of the own defence depends on how often the own team is bypassed - i.e. how many waves of attack potentially roll towards the defence.

The formula is:

"Suffered bypassed defenders (team value with player on the field)" / ("Bypassed opponents (without second balls) (team value with player on the field)" + "Interception - removed opponents" (team value with player on the field)")

When considering a player, the above values are calculated once for all game sequences when the player was lined up and for all games of his team in the competition when the player was not lined up. The goal is to make the influence of a player on the own defence broken of the team measurable (taking into account the potential attacking waves, see above). If the two values (with and without a player in the lineup) differ, the difference between the values is multiplied by a function (not mentioned in detail here), which also takes the number of games into account. The larger the sample size, the higher the certainty that it was due to the influence of the player.

This ratio is normalized here with the competition average (0). If the value is <0, fewer own defenders are bypassed in relation to the team value (player not lined up) and in relation to the competition average (positive). If the value is >0, more own defenders are bypassed in relation to the team value (players not drafted) and in relation to the competition average (positive).
details.meaning: Player's influence on own defence broken, positional play, closing spaces, few bypassed own defenders (last 5 field players) in relation to potential attacking waves of the opponent.

details.label: Suffered Bypassed Players
details.definition: Number of Suffered Bypassed Opponents with the player on the pitch
details.meaning: Influence on the compactness, work rate against the ball, closing defensive spaces, closing passing lines, creating pressure. If a team gets bypassed a lot, a Striker is obviously not having a positive influence on the overall compactness of a team, hence the player has a weaker score

details.label: Influence on Allowed Chances
details.definition:
"Opponent scoring chances" in context to bypassed own defenders as a deviation from the competition average.

"Influence on Allowed Chances" is based on a shot based xG measure, which is based on the number of opponents between the ball and the goal and the pressure on the finishing player. The value is conceptualized so that on average four scoring chances result in one goal. Thus, a shot with an xG value of 0.125 would be half a scoring chance.

Ratio of "Influence on Allowed Chances" (numerator) taken out of play by own defenders (denominator) to see how many chances are allowed when the opponent has already played his way through the defence. The goal here is to capture not only situations cleared by interventions, but also, for example, opponent offensive actions delayed or otherwise prevented by positional play.

The formula includes, among other things, the term:

"Allowed chances (team value with player on the field)" / ("Bypassed defenders (team value with player on the field)" + "Turnover - removed teammates (defenders) (team value with player on the field)"

This ratio is normalized here with the competition average (0). If the value is <0, fewer chances are allowed in relation to own defenders taken out of the game (positive). If the value is >0, more chances are allowed in relation to the own defenders taken out of the game (negative).

If the value is calculated for a player, only game sequences in which the player was lined up are taken into account.
details.meaning: Allowed chances per own defenders bypassed; Chance prevention also through positional play and zonal marking; Allow few scoring chances when own defence is bypassed.

details.label: Ratio - add opponents (without shots)
details.definition:
Offensive ball losses in context to potential possible ball losses, meaningful ratio for the KPI "Ball loss - added opponents"

Ratio of "Ball loss - added opponents" (numerator) to other packing values (denominator) expressing how many opponents could be brought back into play in the first place.

The team-level formula is:

"Ball loss - added opponents (without shots)" / ("Bypassed opponents (without second balls)" +"Interception - removed opponents")

The formula at player level is:

"Ball loss - added opponents (without shots)" / ("Bypassed opponent Receiving" + "Ball win - removed opponents")
details.meaning: Ball control in offensive actions, few ball losses in offensive situations

details.label: Ratio - Backpass add opponents
details.definition:
Back passes/dragging in context to potential possible back pass actions, meaningful ratio for the KPI "Reverse play - added opponents"

Ratio of "Reverse play - added opponents in" (numerator) to other packing values (denominator), expressing how many opponents could be brought back into play in the first place.

The team-level formula is:

"Backpasses - added opponents" / ("Bypassed opponents (without second balls)" + "Offensive intervention - removed opponents")

The formula at player level is:

"Backpasses - added opponents" / ("Bypassed opponents Receiver" + "Offensive intervention - removed opponents")
details.meaning: Ratio of offensively attempted progressive play, few back passes or dragging out of situations

details.label: Total touches FBR
details.definition: Sum of all defensive and offensive touches in the packing zone FBR.
details.meaning: The presence of the player in a certain space.

details.label: Total touches CB
details.definition: Sum of all defensive and offensive touches in the packing zone CB.
details.meaning: The presence of the player in a certain space.

details.label: Total touches FBL
details.definition: Sum of all defensive and offensive touches in the packing zone FBL.
details.meaning: The presence of the player in a certain space.

details.label: Total touches DM
details.definition: Sum of all defensive and offensive touches in the packing zone DM.
details.meaning: The presence of the player in a certain space.

details.label: Total touches WR
details.definition: Sum of all defensive and offensive touches in the packing zone WR.
details.meaning: The presence of the player in a certain space.

details.label: Total touches WL
details.definition: Sum of all defensive and offensive touches in the packing zone WL.
details.meaning: The presence of the player in a certain space.

details.label: Total touches CM
details.definition: Sum of all defensive and offensive touches in the packing zone CM.
details.meaning: The presence of the player in a certain space.

details.label: Total touches AM
details.definition: Sum of all defensive and offensive touches in the packing zone AM.
details.meaning: The presence of the player in a certain space.

details.label: Total touches IBWR
details.definition: Sum of all defensive and offensive touches in the packing zone IBWR.
details.meaning: The presence of the player in a certain space.

details.label: Total touches IBWL
details.definition: Sum of all defensive and offensive touches in the packing zone IBWL.
details.meaning: The presence of the player in a certain space.

details.label: Total touches IB
details.definition: Sum of all defensive and offensive touches in the packing zone IB.
details.meaning: The presence of the player in a certain space.

details.label: Low pass Score
details.definition:
Takes into account all KPIs related to low passes. The pass is played with the foot and the trajectory of the ball during the pass reaches a maximum of chest height. The ball can land with a teammate, an opponent, offside or out of bounds. The aim of the score is to value a player's performance in this skill.

The following KPIs are included:

  • Bypassed Opponents by Action Low Pass
  • Bypassed Defenders by Action Low Pass
  • Assists by Action Low Pass
  • Ball Loss Removed Teammates by Action Low Pass
  • Ball Loss Removed Teammates Defenders by Action Low Pass
  • Unsuccessful Passes by Action Low Pass
  • Successful Passes by Action Low Pass
details.meaning: A score valuing a player's 'low pass' actions.

details.label: Low Cross Score
details.definition:
Takes into account all KPIs related to low crosses. A low cross is a cross into the opponent's box from wide. The ball must not reach more than chest height during it's trajectory, otherwise it is then counted as a high cross. The aim of the score is to value a player's performance in this skill.

The following KPIs are included:

  • Bypassed Opponents by Action Low Cross
  • Bypassed Defenders by Action Low Cross
  • Assists by Action Low Cross
  • Ball Loss Removed Teammates by Action Low Cross
  • Ball Loss Removed Teammates Defenders by Action Low Cross
  • Unsuccessful Passes by Action Low Cross
  • Successful Passes by Action Low Cross
details.meaning: A score valuing a player's 'low cross' actions.

details.label: High Cross Score
details.definition:
Takes into account all KPIs related to high crosses. A high cross is an aerial cross into the opponent's box from wide. The ball must reach chest height at least once during the ball’s trajectory. The aim of the score is to value a player's performance in this skill.

The following KPIs are included:

  • Bypassed Opponents by Action High Cross
  • Bypassed Defenders by Action High Cross
  • Assists by Action High Cross
  • Ball Loss Removed Teammates by Action High Cross
  • Ball Loss Removed Teammates Defenders by Action High Cross
  • Unsuccessful Passes by Action High Cross
  • Successful Passes by Action High Cross
details.meaning: A score valuing a player's 'high cross' actions.

details.label: Diagonal pass Score
details.definition:
Takes into account all KPIs related to diagonal passes. A diagonal ball is a high ball played with the foot from the centre or out wide and switches of play to the wing. It is distinguished from the chipped/ lofted pass, the cross, a clearance and the short aerial pass. The aim of the score is to value a player's performance in this skill.

The following KPIs are included:

  • Bypassed Opponents by Action Diagonal Pass
  • Bypassed Defenders by Action Diagonal Pass
  • Assists by Action Diagonal Pass
  • Ball Loss Removed Teammates by Action Diagonal Pass
  • Ball Loss Removed Teammates Defenders by Action Diagonal Pass
  • Unsuccessful Passes by Action Diagonal Pass
  • Successful Passes by Action Diagonal Pass
details.meaning: A score valuing a player's 'diagonal pass' actions.

details.label: Chipped/Lofted ball Score
details.definition:
Takes into account all KPIs related to chipped or lofted passes. The aim of the score is to value a player's performance in this skill.

The following KPIs are included:

  • Bypassed Opponents by Action Chipped Pass
  • Bypassed Defenders by Action Chipped Pass
  • Assists by Action Chipped Pass
  • Ball Loss Removed Teammates by Action Chipped Pass
  • Ball Loss Removed Teammates Defenders by Action Chipped Pass
  • Unsuccessful Passes by Action Chipped Pass
  • Successful Passes by Action Chipped Pass
details.meaning: A score valuing a player's 'chipped/lofted pass' actions.

details.label: Short aerial pass Score
details.definition:
Takes into account all KPIs related to short aerial passes which are high passes from the foot. These are predominantly short lobs, volleys or chips, e.g. as a result of a throw-in or after a bounce. It differs from the chipped/lofted pass primarily due to the length of the pass (<15 metres). The aim of the score is to value a player's performance in this skill.

The following KPIs are included:

  • Bypassed Opponents by Action Short Aerial Pass
  • Bypassed Defenders by Action Short Aerial Pass
  • Assists by Action Short Aerial Pass
  • Ball Loss Removed Teammates by Action Short Aerial Pass
  • Ball Loss Removed Teammates Defenders by Action Short Aerial Pass
  • Unsuccessful Passes by Action Short Aerial Pass
  • Successful Passes by Action Short Aerial Pass
details.meaning: A score valuing a player's 'short aerial pass' actions.

details.label: Dribble Score
details.definition:
Takes into account all KPIs related to dribbles. A dribble is defined by a player carrying the ball. The aim of the score is to value a player's performance in this skill.

The following KPIs are included:

  • Bypassed Opponents by Action Dribble
  • Bypassed Defenders by Action Dribble
details.meaning: A score valuing a player's 'dribble' actions.

details.label: Availablity Out Wide Score
details.definition:
Takes into account all KPIs related to the action 'availablty of a player out wide'. This action is defined by receiving the ball from a teammate in one of the following zones: Wide Right/Left or Full Back Right/Left. The aim of the score is to value a player's performance in this skill.

The following KPIs are included:

  • Bypassed Opponents Receiving by Action Availability Out Wide
  • Bypassed Defenders Receiving by Action Availability Out Wide
  • Offensive Touches by Action Availability Out Wide
details.meaning: A score valuing a player's 'availabilty out wide'.

details.label: Availability Between the Lines Score
details.definition:
Takes into account all KPIs related to the action 'availablty between the lines'. This action is defined by receiving the ball from a teammate in one of the central midfield zones (DM zone, CM zone and AM zone). The aim of the score is to value a player's performance in this skill.

The following KPIs are included:

  • Bypassed Opponents Receiving by Action Availability Between the Lines
  • Bypassed Defenders Receiving by Action Availability Between the Lines
  • Offensive Touches by Action Availability Between the Lines
details.meaning: A score valuing a player's 'availabilty between the lines'.

details.label: Availability from Deep Runs Score
details.definition:
Takes into account all KPIs realted to the action 'availability from deep runs'. This action is defined by receiving the ball behind the opponent's defensive chain. The ball must therefore be received in one of the three zones behind the defence (in-behind wide right/left or in-behind central), and the pass must be made from one of the zones in front. The aim of the score is to value a player's performance in this skill.

The following KPIs are included:

  • Bypassed Defenders Receiving by Action Availability From Deep Runs
  • Offensive Touches by Action Availability From Deep Runs
details.meaning: A score valuing a player's 'availabilty from deep runs'.

details.label: Availability in the Box Score
details.definition:
Takes into account all KPIs realted to the action 'availability in the box'. This action is defined as receiving the ball within the opponent's box. The aim of the score is to value a player's performance in this skill.

The following KPIs are included:

  • Bypassed Opponents Receiving by Action Availability in the Box
  • Bypassed Defenders Receiving by Action Availability in the Box
  • Offensive Touches by Action Availability in the Box
  • Shot-based xG by Action Close Range Shot
  • Shot-based xG by Action Open Goal Shot
  • Shot-based xG by Action Header
details.meaning: A score valuing a player's 'availability in the box'.

details.label: Hold-Up play Score
details.definition:
Takes into account all KPIs related to high pressure situations or hold-up plays - situations where a player receives a high pass under pressure or a low pass where he has to hold the ball in a one-on-one situation. The aim of the score is to value a player's performance in this skill.

The following KPIs are included:

  • Bypassed Opponents Receiving by Action Hold-Up Play
  • Bypassed Defenders Receiving by Action Hold-Up Play
  • Offensive Touches by Action Hold-up Play
details.meaning: A score valuing a player's 'hold-up play' actions.

details.label: Offensive header Score
details.definition:
Takes into account all KPIs related to offensive actions performed with the head. The score includes all ball passes that are performed with the head and the ball has previously come from a teammate. Goal finishes with the head are excluded. The aim of the score is to value a player's performance in this skill.

The following KPIs are included:

  • Bypassed Opponents by Action Header
  • Bypassed Defenders by Action Header
  • Bypassed Opponents Receiving by Action Header
  • Bypassed Defenders Receiving by Action Header
  • Offensive Touches by Action Header
  • Won Aerial Duels
  • Lost Aerial Duels
details.meaning: A score valuing a player's offensive 'header' actions.

details.label: Defensive Header Score
details.definition:
Takes into account all KPIs related to defensive actions performed with the head. The aim of the score is to value a player's performance in this skill.

The following KPIs are included:

  • Won Aerial Duels
  • Lost Aerial Duels
details.meaning: A score valuing a player's defensive 'header' actions.

details.label: Clearance with foot Score
details.definition:
Takes into account all KPIs related to clearances. A clearance is characterised by the need to relieve a high press situation by clearing the ball with a defensive action. These are most frequently clearances with only one contact on the ball. The aim of the score is to value a player's performance in this skill.

The following KPIs are included:

  • Successful Passes by Action Clearance with Foot
  • Unsuccessful Passes by Action Clearance with Foot
  • Ball Loss Removed Teammates by Action Clearance with Foot
  • Ball Loss Removed Teammates Defenders by Action Clearance with Foot
details.meaning: A score valuing a player's 'clearance' actions.

details.label: Loose ball regain Score
details.definition:
Takes into account all KPIs related to loose ball regains. Loose balls are 'free balls' that are picked up by the player after blocks, aerial duels, failed passes and more. The aim of the score is to value a player's performance in this skill.

The following KPIs are included:

  • Offensive Touches by Action Loose Ball Regain
  • Defensive Touches by Action Loose Ball Regain
  • Ball Win Removed Opponents by Action Loose Ball Regain
  • Ball Win Removed Opponents Defenders by Action Loose Ball Regain
  • Ball Win Added Teammates by Action Loose Ball Regain
  • Ball Win Added Teammates Defenders by Action Loose Ball Regain
details.meaning: A score valuing a player's 'loose ball regain' actions.

details.label: Interception Score
details.definition:
Takes into account all KPIs related to interceptions. Interceptions include all opponent pass attempts that are picked up by the player within the opponent formation. The aim of the score is to value a player's performance in this skill.

The following KPIs are included:

  • Ball Win Number by Action Interception
  • Ball Win Added Teammates by Action Interception
  • Ball Win Added Teammates Defenders by Action Interception
  • Ball Win Removed Opponents by Action Interception
  • Ball Win Removed Opponents Defenders by Action Interception
details.meaning: A score valuing a player's 'interception' actions.

details.label: Ground duel Score
details.definition:
Takes into account all KPIs related to ground duels. Ground duel describes the (temporary) winning of the ball through a duel. The opponent must not have attempted a pass, but have started a dribble. The aim of the score is to value a player's performance in this skill.

The following KPIs are included:

  • Ball Win Removed Opponents by Action Ground Duel
  • Ball Win Removed Opponents Defenders by Action Ground Duel
  • Ball Win Added Teammates by Action Ground Duel
  • Ball Win Added Teammates Defenders by Action Ground Duel
  • Won Ground Duels
  • Lost Ground Duels.
details.meaning: A score valuing a player's 'ground duel' actions.

details.label: Block Score
details.definition:
Takes into account all KPIs related to blocks. An action is considered blocked if a player touches the ball under such high pressure that he has no possibility of controlling the ball (blocking the shot, deflecting it, being shot at, etc.). The aim of the score is to value a player's performance in this skill.

The following KPIs are included:

  • Defensive Touches by Action Block
  • Ball Win Added Teammates by Action Block
details.meaning: A score valuing a player's 'block' actions.

details.label: Long range shot Score
details.definition:
Takes into account all KPIs related to shots from over 22 metres out, measured from the centre of the goal. The aim of the score is to value a player's performance in this skill.

The following KPIs are included:

  • Goals by Action Long Range Shot
  • Shot-based xG by Action Long Range Shot
  • Shot At Goal Number by Action Long Range Shot
  • Shot At Goal Off Target Number by Action Long Range Shot
details.meaning: A score valuing a player's 'long range shot' actions.

details.label: Mid range shot Score
details.definition:
Takes into account all KPIs related to shots from over 10-22 metres out, measured from the centre of the goal. The aim of the score is to value a player's performance in this skill.

The following KPIs are included:

  • Goals by Action Mid Range Shot
  • Shot-based xG by Action Mid Range Shot
  • Shot At Goal Number by Action Mid Range Shot
  • Shot At Goal Off Target Number by Action Mid Range Shot
details.meaning: A score valuing a player's 'mid range shot' actions.

details.label: Close range shot Score
details.definition:
Takes into account all KPIs related to shots from up to 10 metres away, measured to the centre of the goal that are not a clear 1-on-1 against the goalkeeper. The aim of the score is to value a player's performance in this skill.

The following KPIs are included:

  • Goals by Action Close Range Shot
  • Shot-based xG by Action Close Range Shot
  • Shot At Goal Number by Action Close Range Shot
  • Shot At Goal Off Target Number by Action Close Range Shot
details.meaning: A score valuing a player's 'close range shot' actions.

details.label: 1-vs-1 against GK Shot Score
details.definition:
Takes into account all KPIs related to goal-scoring situations in which the shooter is free in front of the goalkeeper or the last man. The aim of the score is to value a player's performance in this skill.

The following KPIs are included:

  • Goals by Action 1vs1 against GK Shot
  • Shot-based xG by Action 1vs1 against GK Shot
  • Shot At Goal Number by Action 1vs1 against GK Shot
  • Shot At Goal Off Target Number by Action 1vs1 against GK Shot
details.meaning: A score valuing a player's '1vs1 against GK shot' actions.

details.label: Open Goal Shot Score
details.definition:
Takes into account all KPIs related to shots, where all opposing players have already been bypassed and the attacker gets to shoot at an open goal. The aim of the score is to value a player's performance in this skill.

The following KPIs are included:

  • Goals by Action Open Goal Shot
  • Shot-based xG by Action Open Goal Shot
  • Shot At Goal Number by Action Open Goal Shot
  • Shot At Goal Off Target Number by Action Open Goal Shot
details.meaning: A score valuing a player's 'open goal shot' actions.

details.label: Header shot Score
details.definition:
Takes into account all KPIs related to goal finishes that are performed with the head. The aim of the score is to value a player's performance in this skill.

The following KPIs are included:

  • Goals by Action Header
  • Shot-based xG by Action Header
  • Shot At Goal Number by Action Header
  • Shot At Goal Off Target Number by Action Header
details.meaning: A score valuing a player's shots from 'header' actions.

details.label: Throw-In Score
details.definition:
Takes into account all KPIs related to the set piece 'throw-in'. The aim of the score is to value a player's performance in this skill.

The following KPIs are included:

  • Bypassed Opponents by Action Throw-In
  • Bypassed Defenders by Action Throw-In
  • Assists by Action Throw-In
  • Successful Passes by Action Throw-In
  • Unsuccessful Passes by Action Throw-In
details.meaning: A score valuing a player's 'throw-in' actions.

details.label: Corner Score
details.definition:
Takes into account all KPIs related to the set piece 'corner'. The aim of the score is to value a player's performance in this skill.

The following KPIs are included:

  • Bypassed Opponents by Action Corner
  • Bypassed Defenders by Action Corner
  • Assists by Action Corner
  • Successful Passes by Action Corner
  • Unsuccessful Passes by Action Corner
details.meaning: A score valuing a player's 'corner' actions.

details.label: Free kick Score
details.definition:
Takes into account all KPIs related to the set piece 'free kick'. The aim of the score is to value a player's performance in this skill.

The following KPIs are included:

  • Bypassed Opponents by Action Free Kick
  • Bypassed Defenders by Action Free Kick
  • Assists by Action Free Kick
  • Successful Passes by Action Free Kick
  • Unsuccessful Passes by Action Free Kick
details.meaning: A score valuing a player's 'free kick' actions.

details.label: Penalty Score
details.definition:
Takes into account all KPIs related to the set piece 'penalty'. The aim of the score is to value a player's performance in this skill.

The following KPIs are included:

  • Goals by Action Penalty
  • Shot-based xG by Action Penalty
details.meaning: A score valuing a player's 'penalty' actions.

details.label: Goal kick Score
details.definition:
Takes into account all KPIs related to the set piece 'goal kick'. The aim of the score is to value a player's performance in this skill.

The following KPIs are included:

  • Bypassed Opponents by Action Goal Kick
  • Bypassed Defenders by Action Goal Kick
  • Assists by Action Goal Kick
  • Successful Passes by Action Goal Kick
  • Unsuccessful Passes by Action Goal Kick
details.meaning: A score valuing a player's 'free kick' actions.

details.label: Aerial Duel Success Rate
details.definition:
The percentage of successful aerial duels in relation to the total number of aerial duels.

Formula:

Won Aerial Duels / (Won Aerial Duels + Lost Aerial Duels
details.meaning: Success rate of a player in aerial duels.

details.label: Number of Aerial Duels
details.definition: Number of Aerial Duels
details.meaning: Number of Aerial Duels

details.label: Ground Duel Success Rate
details.definition:
The percentage of successful ground duels in relation to the total number of ground duels.

Formula:

Won Ground Duels / (Won Ground Duels + Lost Ground Duels)
details.meaning: Success rate of a player in ground duels.

details.label: Minutes / Goal
details.definition: Minutes to Goal Ratio
details.meaning: Minutes to Goal Ratio

details.label: Minutes / shot-based xG
details.definition: Minutes to Expected Goals Ratio
details.meaning: How long does a player take to accumulate an expected goals value of 1, based on on their shooting chances. This shows how often a player gets into good shooting areas & gets their shot away, regardless of if they manage to finish their chance.

details.label: Ratio Goals / shot-based xG
details.definition:
Goals scored in relation to shot-based xG.

Formula: goals / shot-based xG

The value is intended to represent the goal-scoring efficiency of a player or a team.A value above 1, indicates that a player or a team has scored more goals than expected based on their goal finishes.A value below 1 indicates that a player or team has scored fewer goals than expected based on their goal finishes.

This score does not indicate whether it resulted because of the quality of the finish or the goalkeeping. A better measurement of this is made with post-shot xG value..
details.meaning: Measures the overperformance of goals scored, compared to the chance the goal results from. There is no consideration of the likely point of the ball in the goal as it would cross the line, so this value can depend on the quality of a player's finishing or on the shot-stopping of the goalkeeper.

details.label: Ratio Shots on target
details.definition:
The ratio of all shots to those that are on target.

Formula: shots on goal / total shots
details.meaning: Shows how often shots taken actually end up on target and how efficient a team or player is at hitting the target with their shots.

details.label: Ratio Shots / Goal
details.definition: The ratio of total shots to goals.
details.meaning: Shows how often shots taken actually end up being converted & how efficient a team/ shooter is at finishing chances. Can also be a sign of taking multiple low quality shots if the ratio is poor.

details.label: Minutes / Assist
details.definition: Minutes per assist.
details.meaning: Minutes per assist.

details.label: Total Touches
details.definition: Sum of all events in defensive play (directly after the opposition is in possession) and events in offensive play (whilst team is in possession).
details.meaning: Number of total touches of a player.

details.label: Pass success rate
details.definition:
The percentage of successful passes in relation to the total number of passes excluding neutral passes.

The following types of passes are considered:
  • Low pass
  • Diagonal pass
  • Chipped/Lofted Pass

    Neutral passes are difficult-to-control (>=80% pressure) balls that go to a teammate or opponent. These balls are not taken into account in the passing success rate, as they are neither considered as clearly received nor as clearly missed passes. Mostly these are long balls into aerial duels. Neutral passes also include passes that were interrupted by a foul.

    Formula:

    Successful Passes / (Successful Passes + Unsuccessful Passes)
details.meaning: Can help illustrate how safe a player plays their passes &/or their passing ability.

details.label: Number of Ground Duels
details.definition: Number of Ground Duels
details.meaning: Number of Ground Duels

details.label: Ratio postshotxG / xG
details.definition:
Post-shot xG (including impact point of the ball in the goal) in relation to the starting point of the shot on goal (shot-based xG).

Formula: post-shot xG / shot-based xG

This is intended to represent the pure finishing quality of a player or a team.A value above 1 indicates that a player or a team generates a greater goal scoring probability from goal scoring chances through their good finishing.A value below 1 indiciates that a player or a team generates a smaller goal scoring probability from goal scoring chances through their poor finishing.

details.label: Ratio Goals / post-shot xG
details.definition:
Goals scored in relation to post-shot xG.

Formula: Goals / post-shot xG

Goalkeeper's perspective:

The value gives a statement about the goalkeeping performance after shots at goal. A value below 1 shows that the goalkeeper saved his team from conceding goals because he conceded fewer goals than what was expected on the basis of the opponent's finishing. If the value is above 1, it means that less goals were expected on the basis of the opponent's finishing.

Shooter's perspective:

The value gives a statement about the finishing quality in combination with a average opponent goalkeeping performance. A value above 1 indicates an above-average shooting performance or below-average opponent goalkeeping performance. If the value is below 1, it means that the finishing quality was either below average or the opposing goalkeeping performance was above average.
details.meaning: Delivers a statement about the finishing quality of a player and the opponent's goalkeeping performance. A high ratio indicates either a high finishing quality of the striker or a poor opponent's goalkeeping performance.

details.label: Successful Passes*
details.definition:
A successful pass is any pass that reaches a teammate. In addition, the teammate must have be able to control the ball or the pressure on the teammate must not be too high. E.g. if a striker is given an aerial duel to compete for, this would be counted as a 'neutral ball'.

*Only some types of passes

The following types of passes are considered:
  • Low pass
  • Diagonal pass
  • Chipped/Lofted ball
details.meaning: Pass that reaches a teammate.

details.label: Unsuccessful Passes*
details.definition:
An unsuccessful pass is any pass that is intercepted by an opponent or goes out of play or offside. In addition, the opponent intercepting the ball must be able to control the ball or the pressure must not be too high. E.g. if a long ball is played and there is an aerial duel, it would be a "neutral ball" - regardless of who wins this duel.

*Only some types of passes

The following types of passes are considered:
  • Low pass
  • Diagonal pass
  • Chipped/Lofted ball
details.meaning: Pass that goes to the opponent, out of play or offside.

details.label: Prevented Goals Total (based on shot-based xG)
details.definition: Total amount of goals conceeded in relation to the opponents post-shot xG. Shot-based expected goals (xG) is a figure that adds a value to all shot attempts. This includes shots from open-play, direct free kicks and penalty kicks. In order to generate a figure that values probability of a goal being scored from a shot, parameters such as shot distance, shot angle, pressure on the player, opponent between the ball and the goal, body part when the ball is released from and the respective match situation are taken into account.
details.meaning: The value puts the expected oppositions's expected goals on the basis of their scoring opportunities in relation to the actually conceded goals. It therefore indicates how many goals the goalkeeper prevented.

details.label: Prevented Goals Percent (based on shot-based xG)
details.definition: Total amount of goals conceeded in relation to the opponents post-shot xG in percentage. Shot-based expected goals (xG) is a figure that adds a value to all shot attempts. This includes shots from open-play, direct free kicks and penalty kicks. In order to generate a figure that values probability of a goal being scored from a shot, parameters such as shot distance, shot angle, pressure on the player, opponent between the ball and the goal, body part when the ball is released from and the respective match situation are taken into account.
details.meaning: The value puts the oppositions's expected goals on the basis of their scoring opportunities in relation to the actually conceded goals. It therefore indicates how many goals the goalkeeper prevented in percent.

details.label: Prevented Goals Total (based on post-shot xG)
details.definition: Total amount of goals conceeded in relation to the opponents post-shot xG. Post-shot xG is a value of all shots on target (all shots on goal that go in or are saved by the goalkeeper). In addition to the shot-based xG variables (shot distance, shot angle, pressure on the shooter, opponents between ball and goal, phase of play, and type of shot), the "likely finishing position" of the ball as it crosses the goal line is also used to create the post-shot xG value. All shots that are blocked, hit the post, crossbar or go out of bounds are not taken into account and are given a value of 0.
details.meaning: The value puts the oppositions's expected goals on the basis of their scoring opportunities including the point of impact in goal in relation to the actually conceded goals. It therefore indicates how many goals the goalkeeper prevented.

details.label: Prevented Goals Percent (based on post-shot xG)
details.definition: Total amount of goals conceeded in relation to the opponents post-shot xG in percentage. Post-shot xG is a value of all shots on target (all shots on goal that go in or are saved by the goalkeeper). In addition to the shot-based xG variables (shot distance, shot angle, pressure on the shooter, opponents between ball and goal, phase of play, and type of shot), the "likely finishing position" of the ball as it crosses the goal line is also used to create the post-shot xG value. All shots that are blocked, hit the post, crossbar or go out of bounds are not taken into account and are given a value of 0.
details.meaning: The value puts the oppositions's expected goals on the basis of their scoring opportunities including the point of impact in goal in relation to the actually conceded goals. It therefore indicates how many goals the goalkeeper prevented in percent.

details.label: Defensive Touches outside the Box per game
details.definition: Total number of defensive touches outside of the box

details.label: Caught Balls Percent
details.definition: Total number of high balls caught in relation to the oppositions's total number of high balls. High balls happen after free kicks in the final third, corners and high crosses.

details.label: Caught and Punched Balls Percent
details.definition: Total number of high balls saved and caught in relation to the oppositions's total number of high balls. High balls occur after free kicks in the final third, corners and high crosses.

details.label: Successful Launches Percent per game
details.definition:
Ratio of successful goal kicks to all goal kicks.

the formula is:

Successful Goal Kicks / (Successful Goal Kicks + Unsuccessful Goal Kicks)
details.meaning: Provides you with a statement about the quality of the goalkeeper's goal kicks. The higher the % value, the better.

details.label: Number of Aerial Duels in Packing Zone CB
details.definition: Number of Aerial Duels in Packing Zone CB
details.meaning: Number of Aerial Duels in Packing Zone CB

details.label: IMPECT PXT
details.definition:
Influence of a player on the change in own goal danger and counter-goal danger.

This score is calculated by adding the player's IMPECT in the offensive and the defensive phase:
IMPECT PXT = Offensive IMPECT PXT + Defensive IMPECT PXT
details.meaning:
Overall performance, effectiveness of a player.

Each action of a player is assessed according to how much it changes the probability of scoring or conceding goals.

All actions (on- and off-ball) are totalled and summarised in one value.

details.label: Offensive IMPECT PXT
details.definition: The Offensive IMPECT (PXT) explains the influence of a player while the own team is in the attacking phase (‘Attacking Squad’). This influence consists of the change in your own scoring threat and the change in the opponent's threat.
details.meaning: Evaluates the performance of a player while the own team is attacking.

details.label: Defensive IMPECT PXT
details.definition: The Defensive IMPECT PXT explains the influence of a player while the own team is not in the attacking phase (‘Attacking Squad’). This influence consists of the change in your own scoring threat and the change in the opponent's threat.
details.meaning: Evaluates the performance of a player while the own team is defending.

details.definition:
The change in a player's own goal threat based on the Packing Expected Threat Model (PXT). All actions that can be attributed to a player are analysed. These include Shots, passes, dribbles, standards, progressive ball receptions, ball recoveries, fouls called and own fouls, blocks and defensive spatial behaviour.
In the case of progressive passes, 50% of the increase in scoring danger is allocated to the passer (PXT_PASS_PRO) and 50% to the pass receiver (PXT_REC). Justification: A good pass necessarily requires two players, so this value must be divided up so that all the total player values are equal to the team value.

The formula is PXT = 0.5 x PXT_PASS_PRO + 0.5 x PXT_SETPIECE_PRO + 0.5 x PXT_REC + PXT_PASS_FAIL + PXT_PASS_REG + PXT_SETPIECE_FAIL + PXT_SETPIECE_REG + PXT_DRIBBLE+PXT_BLOCK+PXT_SHOT+PXT_BALL_WIN+PXT_FOUL+PXT_PASSIVE+PXT_FOULED

The alternative formula is: PXT = PXT_ATTACK + PXT_DEFEND

The change in the opponent's scoring threat by a player based on the Packing Expected Threat Model (PXT). All actions that can be attributed to a player either through active actions or passive actions are analysed. This includes changes caused by active actions such as winning the ball, losing the ball, but also passive actions in which a passive player (off-ball) is held responsible for the opponent's change in goal danger. For this purpose, the responsability model is used, which distributes the change in goal risk to the players based on the packing zone and the team formation. If a player allows opposing passes, set pieces or dribbles into a packing zone for which he is responsible, then he passively increases the risk of conceding - conversely, he can also force the opponent into regressive actions and reduce the risk of conceding by successfully defending the zone.

The formula is: DEF_PXT = DEF_PXT_PASS + DEF_PXT_DRIBBLE + DEF_PXT_SETPIECE + DEF_PXT_BLOCK + DEF_PXT_BALL_WIN + DEF_PXT_BALL_LOSS + DEF_PXT_FOUL + DEF_PXT_ACTIVE

The alternative formula is: DEF_PXT = DEF_PXT_ATTACK + DEF_PXT_DEFEND
details.meaning:
Evaluates the influence of a player on the danger of goals conceded by his team. This key figure has the unit goals conceded.

A value of -0.2 indicates that the player reduces the danger of conceding goals by 0.20 goals.

Example: If a centre-back has a critical ball loss in the build-up, this is included negatively in the Defensive Packing Expected Threat value. The reason for this is that the probability of conceding a goal has increased.

details.label: IMPECT PXT (w/o fouls)
details.definition:
Influence of a player on the change in own goal danger and counter-goal danger with fouls commited or won being excluded.

This score is calculated by adding the player's IMPECT in the offensive and the defensive phase:
IMPECT PXT (w/o fouls) = Offensive IMPECT PXT (w/o fouls) + Defensive IMPECT PXT (w/o fouls)
details.meaning:
Overall performance, effectiveness of a player with fouls commited and won being excluded.

Each action (except for fouls commited and won) of a player is assessed according to how much it changes the probability of scoring or conceding goals.

All actions except for fouls commited and won (on- and off-ball) are totalled and summarised in one value.

details.label: Offensive IMPECT PXT (w/o fouls)
details.definition: The Offensive IMPECT PXT (w/o fouls) explains the influence of a player while the own team is in the attacking phase (‘Attacking Squad’) with fouls committed and won being excluded. This influence consists of the change in your own scoring threat and the change in the opponent's threat.
details.meaning: Evaluates the performance of a player while the own team is attacking with fouls (commited or won) being excluded.

details.label: Defensive IMPECT PXT (w/o fouls)
details.definition: The Defensive IMPECT PXT (w/o fouls) explains the influence of a player while the own team is not in the attacking phase (‘Attacking Squad’) with fouls committed and won being excluded. This influence consists of the change in your own scoring threat and the change in the opponent's threat.
details.meaning: Evaluates the performance of a player while the own team is defending with fouls (commited or won) being excluded.

details.label: IMPECT PXT (w/o shots)
details.definition:
Influence of a player on the change in own goal danger and counter-goal danger with shots being excluded.

This score is calculated by adding the player's IMPECT in the offensive and the defensive phase:
IMPECT PXT (w/o shots) = Offensive IMPECT PXT (w/o shots) + Defensive IMPECT PXT (w/o shots)
details.meaning:
Overall performance, effectiveness of a player with shots being excluded.

Each action (except for shots) of a player is assessed according to how much it changes the probability of scoring or conceding goals.

All actions except for shots (on- and off-ball) are totalled and summarised in one value.

details.label: Offensive IMPECT PXT (w/o shots)
details.definition: The Offensive IMPECT PXT (w/o shots) explains the influence of a player while the own team is in the attacking phase (‘Attacking Squad’) with shots being excluded. This influence consists of the change in your own scoring threat and the change in the opponent's threat.
details.meaning: Evaluates the performance of a player while the own team is attacking with shots being excluded.

details.label: Defensive IMPECT PXT (w/o shots)
details.definition: The Defensive IMPECT PXT (w/o shots) explains the influence of a player while the own team is not in the attacking phase (‘Attacking Squad’) with shots being excluded. This influence consists of the change in your own scoring threat and the change in the opponent's threat.
details.meaning: Evaluates the performance of a player while the own team is defending with shots being excluded.

details.label: IMPECT PXT (w/o set pieces)
details.definition:
Influence of a player on the change in own goal danger and counter-goal danger with set pieces being excluded.

This score is calculated by adding the player's IMPECT in the offensive and the defensive phase:
IMPECT PXT (w/o set pieces) = Offensive IMPECT PXT (w/o set pieces) + Defensive IMPECT PXT (w/o set pieces)
details.meaning:
Overall performance, effectiveness of a player with set pieces being excluded.

Each action (except for set pieces) of a player is assessed according to how much it changes the probability of scoring or conceding goals.

All actions except for set pieces (on- and off-ball) are totalled and summarised in one value.

details.label: Offensive IMPECT PXT (w/o set pieces)
details.definition: The Offensive IMPECT PXT (w/o set pieces) explains the influence of a player while the own team is in the attacking phase (‘Attacking Squad’) with set pieces being excluded. This influence consists of the change in your own scoring threat and the change in the opponent's threat.
details.meaning: Evaluates the performance of a player while the own team is attacking with set pieces being excluded.

details.label: Defensive IMPECT PXT (w/o set pieces)
details.definition: The Defensive IMPECT PXT (w/o set pieces) explains the influence of a player while the own team is not in the attacking phase (‘Attacking Squad’) with set pieces being excluded. This influence consists of the change in your own scoring threat and the change in the opponent's threat.
details.meaning: Evaluates the performance of a player while the own team is defending with set pieces being excluded.

details.label: IMPECT PXT (w/o fouls and shots)
details.definition:
Influence of a player on the change in own goal danger and counter-goal danger with fouls commited and won as well as shots being excluded.

This score is calculated by adding the player's IMPECT in the offensive and the defensive phase:
IMPECT PXT (w/o fouls and shots) = Offensive IMPECT PXT (w/o fouls and shots) + Defensive IMPECT PXT (w/o fouls and shots)
details.meaning:
Overall performance, effectiveness of a player with fouls commited and won as well as shots being excluded.

Each action (except for fouls commited and won as well as shots) of a player is assessed according to how much it changes the probability of scoring or conceding goals.

All actions except for fouls commited and won as well as shots (on- and off-ball) are totalled and summarised in one value.

details.label: Offensive IMPECT PXT (w/o fouls and shots)
details.definition: The Offensive IMPECT PXT (w/o fouls and shots) explains the influence of a player while the own team is in the attacking phase (‘Attacking Squad’) with fouls committed and won as well as shots being excluded. This influence consists of the change in your own scoring threat and the change in the opponent's threat.
details.meaning: Evaluates the performance of a player while the own team is attacking with fouls (commited or won) and shots being excluded.

details.label: Defensive IMPECT PXT (w/o fouls and shots)
details.definition: The Defensive IMPECT PXT (w/o fouls and shots) explains the influence of a player while the own team is not in the attacking phase (‘Attacking Squad’) with fouls committed and won as well as shots being excluded. This influence consists of the change in your own scoring threat and the change in the opponent's threat.
details.meaning: Evaluates the performance of a player while the own team is defending with fouls (commited or won) and shots being excluded.

details.label: IMPECT PXT (w/o fouls and set pieces)
details.definition:
Influence of a player on the change in own goal danger and counter-goal danger with fouls commited and won as well as set pieces being excluded.

This score is calculated by adding the player's IMPECT in the offensive and the defensive phase:
IMPECT PXT (w/o fouls and set pieces) = Offensive IMPECT PXT (w/o fouls and set pieces) + Defensive IMPECT PXT (w/o fouls and set pieces)
details.meaning:
Overall performance, effectiveness of a player with fouls commited and won as well as set pieces being excluded.

Each action (except for fouls commited and won as well as set pieces) of a player is assessed according to how much it changes the probability of scoring or conceding goals.

All actions except for fouls commited and won as well as set pieces (on- and off-ball) are totalled and summarised in one value.

details.label: Offensive IMPECT PXT (w/o fouls and set pieces)
details.definition: The Offensive IMPECT PXT (w/o fouls and set pieces) explains the influence of a player while the own team is in the attacking phase (‘Attacking Squad’) with fouls committed and won as well as set pieces being excluded. This influence consists of the change in your own scoring threat and the change in the opponent's threat.
details.meaning: Evaluates the performance of a player while the own team is attacking with fouls (commited or won) and set pieces being excluded.

details.label: Defensive IMPECT PXT (w/o fouls and set pieces)
details.definition: The Defensive IMPECT PXT (w/o fouls and set pieces) explains the influence of a player while the own team is not in the attacking phase (‘Attacking Squad’) with fouls committed and won as well as set pieces being excluded. This influence consists of the change in your own scoring threat and the change in the opponent's threat.
details.meaning: Evaluates the performance of a player while the own team is defending with fouls (commited or won) and set pieces being excluded.

details.label: IMPECT PXT (w/o shots and set pieces)
details.definition:
Influence of a player on the change in own goal danger and counter-goal danger with shots and set pieces being excluded.

This score is calculated by adding the player's IMPECT in the offensive and the defensive phase:
IMPECT PXT (w/o shots and set pieces) = Offensive IMPECT PXT (w/o shots and set pieces) + Defensive IMPECT PXT (w/o shots and set pieces)
details.meaning:
Overall performance, effectiveness of a player with shots and set pieces being excluded.

Each action (except for shots and set pieces) of a player is assessed according to how much it changes the probability of scoring or conceding goals.

All actions except for shots and set pieces (on- and off-ball) are totalled and summarised in one value.

details.label: Offensive IMPECT PXT (w/o shots and set pieces)
details.definition: The Offensive IMPECT PXT (w/o shots and set pieces) explains the influence of a player while the own team is in the attacking phase (‘Attacking Squad’) with shots as well as set pieces being excluded. This influence consists of the change in your own scoring threat and the change in the opponent's threat.
details.meaning: Evaluates the performance of a player while the own team is attacking with shots and set pieces being excluded.

details.label: Defensive IMPECT PXT (w/o shots and set pieces)
details.definition: The Defensive IMPECT PXT (w/o shots and set pieces) explains the influence of a player while the own team is not in the attacking phase (‘Attacking Squad’) with shots as well as set pieces being excluded. This influence consists of the change in your own scoring threat and the change in the opponent's threat.
details.meaning: Evaluates the performance of a player while the own team is defending with shots and set pieces being excluded.

details.label: IMPECT PXT (robust)
details.definition:
Influence of a player on the change in own goal danger and counter-goal danger with fouls (commited and won), shots and set pieces being excluded.

This score is calculated by adding the player's IMPECT in the offensive and the defensive phase:
IMPECT PXT (robust) = Offensive IMPECT PXT (robust) + Defensive IMPECT PXT (robust)
details.meaning:
Overall performance, effectiveness of a player with fouls (commited and won), shots and set pieces being excluded.

Each action (except for fouls (commited and won), shots and set pieces) of a player is assessed according to how much it changes the probability of scoring or conceding goals.

All actions except for fouls (commited and won), shots and set pieces (on- and off-ball) are totalled and summarised in one value.

details.label: Offensive IMPECT PXT (robust)
details.definition: The Offensive IMPECT PXT (robust) explains the influence of a player while the own team is in the attacking phase (‘Attacking Squad’) with fouls committed and won as well as set pieces and shots being excluded. This influence consists of the change in your own scoring threat and the change in the opponent's threat.
details.meaning: Evaluates the performance of a player while the own team is attacking with fouls (commited or won), shots and set pieces being excluded.

details.label: Defensive IMPECT PXT (robust)
details.definition: The Defensive IMPECT PXT (robust) explains the influence of a player while the own team is not in the attacking phase (‘Attacking Squad’) with fouls committed and won as well as set pieces and shots being excluded. This influence consists of the change in your own scoring threat and the change in the opponent's threat.
details.meaning: Evaluates the performance of a player while the own team is defending with fouls (commited or won), shots and set pieces being excluded.

details.label: Aerial Duel Defensive Success Rate
details.definition: Aerial Duel Win Ratio Defensive measures the percentage of defensive aerial duels a player successfully wins. It is calculated by dividing the number of won defensive aerial duels by the total number of defensive aerial duels contested (won + lost). The context of each duel is determined by phase of play and team possession status, ensuring only defensive scenarios are included.
details.meaning: This KPI reflects a player’s efficiency in defensive aerial battles. Rather than showing the absolute number of duels won or lost, it highlights how often the player comes out on top when contesting headers in defensive phases. A high value indicates consistent aerial dominance in defense, while a lower value may suggest vulnerability in the air. It is especially useful for evaluating central defenders, defensive midfielders, and full-backs responsible for defending crosses, long balls, or set-pieces.

details.label: Aerial Duel Offensive Success Rate
details.definition: Aerial Duel Offensive Success Rate measures the percentage of offensive aerial duels a player successfully wins. It is calculated by dividing the number of won offensive aerial duels by the total number of offensive aerial duels contested (won + lost). Duels are classified as offensive based on team possession and phase of play to ensure only attacking scenarios are included.
details.meaning: This KPI highlights a player’s aerial effectiveness in attacking contexts. Rather than focusing on volume, it evaluates how often the player wins when engaging in aerial battles to extend or maintain attacking phases. A high success rate reflects good timing, strength, and positioning in the air, and is especially relevant for target strikers, attacking midfielders, and wingers who rely on aerial duels to create second-ball situations or win long balls.

details.label: Ground Duel Defensive Success Rate
details.definition: Ground Duel Defensive Success Rate expresses the share of defensive ground duels a player wins. The value is computed by dividing the number of defensive ground duels won by the total number of defensive ground duels contested. Only duels occurring while the team is out of possession or in a defensive phase are counted, ensuring the KPI reflects true defensive actions.
details.meaning: This KPI provides insight into a player’s reliability in stopping opponents on the ground. It shifts the focus from duel volume to success efficiency, helping to identify defenders and defensive midfielders who consistently win their individual battles. A high success rate indicates good timing, tackling technique, and positioning. It is valuable for assessing 1v1 defending skills and pressure resistance.

details.label: Ground Duel Offensive Success Rate
details.definition: Ground Duel Offensive Success Rate measures how often a player successfully wins ground duels in offensive situations. The ratio is derived by dividing the number of won offensive ground duels by the total contested in offensive phases. Only duels initiated during team possession or attacking sequences are included in the calculation.
details.meaning: This KPI reflects a player’s capacity to hold off defenders, retain possession, or dribble past opponents in contested ground situations. A high rate suggests technical ability and physical robustness in tight attacking spaces. It is especially relevant for wingers, attacking midfielders, and strikers who operate under pressure and rely on duel success to progress play and create scoring opportunities.

details.label: Foot Usage Ratio Right
details.definition: The Foot Usage Ratio Right measures how evenly a player uses both feet during a match or over a period of time. It is calculated as the number of passes made with the right foot divided by the total number of passes with both feet. Only touches tagged with LEFT_FOOT or RIGHT_FOOT are considered - other body parts or ambiguous tags are excluded.
details.meaning: This KPI indicates a player’s level of two-footedness in practical match scenarios. A value close to 0.5 suggests balanced foot usage and a high degree of ambidexterity, while values closer to 0/1 imply a strong preference for one foot. It is especially relevant for evaluating press resistance, passing flexibility, and versatility in tight spaces.

details.label: Foot Usage Ratio Left
details.definition: The Foot Usage Ratio Left measures how evenly a player uses both feet during a match or over a period of time. It is calculated as the number of passes made with the left foot divided by the total number of passes with both feet. Only touches tagged with LEFT_FOOT or RIGHT_FOOT are considered - other body parts or ambiguous tags are excluded.
details.meaning: This KPI indicates a player’s level of two-footedness in practical match scenarios. A value close to 0.5 suggests balanced foot usage and a high degree of ambidexterity, while values closer to 0/1 imply a strong preference for one foot. It is especially relevant for evaluating press resistance, passing flexibility, and versatility in tight spaces.

details.label: Pass Success Rate Right Foot
details.definition: The percentage of passes completed successfully with the right foot, considering only passes classified as LOW_PASS, DIAGONAL_PASS, or CHIPPED_PASS. It is calculated as the ratio of successful right-foot passes of these types divided by the total right-foot attempts of the same types.
details.meaning: This KPI reflects a player’s technical precision when using the right foot for core pass types. A higher value indicates greater reliability and accuracy in right-foot passing, offering insight into foot dominance, technical balance, and potential limitations in distribution.

details.label: Pass Success Rate Left Foot
details.definition: The percentage of passes completed successfully with the left foot, considering only passes classified as LOW_PASS, DIAGONAL_PASS, or CHIPPED_PASS. It is calculated as the ratio of successful left-foot passes of these types divided by the total left-foot attempts of the same types.
details.meaning: This KPI measures a player’s passing efficiency with the left foot for standard pass types. A higher ratio suggests competence and trustworthiness in left-foot passing, while a lower ratio may highlight reliance on the dominant foot or weaknesses in two-footed play.

details.label: Pass Completion Over Expected
details.definition: Pass Completion Over Expected measures the relative difference between the number of passes a player or team successfully completes and the number of passes expected to be completed based on model probabilities (xPass). It quantifies how much a player or team outperforms or underperforms the statistical expectation of pass success. The value is expressed as a percentage above or below the expected baseline. It is calculated as: (SUCCESSFUL_PASSES - EXPECTED_PASSES) / EXPECTED_PASSES
details.meaning: A positive value indicates that the player or team completes more passes than expected, showing technical precision or press resistance. A value close to zero suggests performance in line with the difficulty-adjusted expectation. Negative values highlight underperformance, meaning fewer passes were completed than the model predicted. If a player was expected to complete 20 passes but actually completed 25, then the Pass Completion Over Expected is (25 – 20) / 20 = +25%.

details.label: Passing Accuracy Over Expected
details.definition: Pass Accuracy Over Expected measures how much a player’s actual pass accuracy differs from the accuracy predicted by model probabilities (xPass). It compares successful passes to expected passes, normalized by the total number of attempted passes. The result indicates whether a player’s completion rate is above, below, or in line with expectation given the difficulty of their pass profile. It is calculated as: (SUCCESSFUL_PASSES - EXPECTED_PASSES) / (SUCCESSFUL_PASSES + UNSUCCESSFUL_PASSES)
details.meaning: A positive value shows that the player’s actual pass accuracy is higher than expected, reflecting technical precision or decision-making under pressure. A value close to zero suggests the player performs in line with the expected baseline. Negative values highlight lower-than-expected accuracy, signaling inefficiency or mistakes in execution. For example, if a player’s expected pass accuracy is 80% but their actual accuracy is 90%, the Pass Accuracy Over Expected is +10%.

details.label: Passes*
details.definition: The change in own goal threat through passes, as well as the change in opponent goal threat caused by turnovers during the attacking phase. This includes all progressive passes (which increase goal threat), regressive passes (which reach a teammate but reduce goal threat), and misplaced passes (to an opponent, out of play, or into offside).
details.meaning: Change in own and opponent goal threat caused by passing during the team's attacking phase.

details.label: Ball Wins*
details.definition: Summarizes the change in both own goal threat and opponent goal threat resulting from offensive and defensive ball wins during the attacking phase.
details.meaning: Change in goal threat and opponent goal threat resulting from ball wins during the team's attacking phase.

details.label: Opp. Passes*
details.definition: A player's defensive off-ball effectiveness describes their performance without the ball, especially in spatial defending. It captures how the opponent’s passes — progressive (more dangerous) or regressive (less dangerous) — change the goal threat. This change is distributed among defenders using the Responsibility Model, based on packing zones, i.e., the areas a player is mainly responsible for (e.g., a center-back covering the central space behind the defensive line). If the opponent plays a progressive pass into that zone, the responsible player is negatively rated. If the player forces a regressive pass out of their zone, they receive a positive rating.
details.meaning: Change in goal threat and opponent goal threat caused by opponent passes during their attacking phase.

details.label: Ball Wins*
details.definition: Summarizes the increase in own goal threat and the reduction of opponent goal threat resulting from ball wins during the opponent’s attacking phase.
details.meaning: Change in goal threat and opponent goal threat resulting from ball wins during the opponent’s attacking phase.
